clinical_reason: Severe risk of acute hepatotoxicity (liver damage) due to accidental overlapping overdoses.
text: Primary Medicine: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen). Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Other Acetaminophen-containing cold/flu products, chronic heavy Alcohol consumption. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Severe risk of acute hepatotoxicity (liver damage) due to accidental overlapping overdoses.

clinical_reason: Exponentially increases the risk of gastrointestinal bleeding, stomach ulceration, and severe mucosal damage.
text: Primary Medicine: Ibuprofen / Naproxen / Diclofenac (NSAIDs). Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Aspirin, Oral Corticosteroids (Prednisolone), Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors (SSRIs). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Exponentially increases the risk of gastrointestinal bleeding, stomach ulceration, and severe mucosal damage.

clinical_reason: NSAIDs blunt the therapeutic effectiveness of blood pressure lowering drugs and raise the risk of acute kidney injury.
text: Primary Medicine: Ibuprofen / Naproxen. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Antihypertensives (Losartan, Amlodipine, Metoprolol), Diuretics (Hydrochlorothiazide). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: NSAIDs blunt the therapeutic effectiveness of blood pressure lowering drugs and raise the risk of acute kidney injury.

clinical_reason: Significantly increases the risk of Serotonin Syndrome (a life-threatening crisis) and lowers the seizure threshold.
text: Primary Medicine: Tramadol. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs (Antidepressants), or other serotonergic agents. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Significantly increases the risk of Serotonin Syndrome (a life-threatening crisis) and lowers the seizure threshold.

clinical_reason: Suppressed stomach acidity severely reduces the solubility and systemic absorption of medications that require an acidic gastric environment to work.
text: Primary Medicine: Omeprazole / Pantoprazole (PPIs). Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Ketoconazole, Itraconazole, certain antiretroviral therapies. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Suppressed stomach acidity severely reduces the solubility and systemic absorption of medications that require an acidic gastric environment to work.

avoid_with: Antacids taken at the exact same time
clinical_reason: Can reduce the absorption of the H2-blocker; space administration out by at least 1-2 hours.
text: Primary Medicine: Famotidine / Ranitidine. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Antacids taken at the exact same time. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Can reduce the absorption of the H2-blocker; space administration out by at least 1-2 hours.

clinical_reason: Co-administration with apomorphine can cause severe hypotension and loss of consciousness; other combinations risk cardiac arrhythmias.
text: Primary Medicine: Ondansetron. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Apomorphine, Amiodarone, or other QT-prolonging drugs. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Co-administration with apomorphine can cause severe hypotension and loss of consciousness; other combinations risk cardiac arrhythmias.

clinical_reason: P-glycoprotein inhibitors can drastically increase loperamide concentrations, risking central nervous system toxicity and cardiac arrhythmias.
text: Primary Medicine: Loperamide. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Quinidine, Ritonavir, Gemfibrozil. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: P-glycoprotein inhibitors can drastically increase loperamide concentrations, risking central nervous system toxicity and cardiac arrhythmias.

clinical_reason: Additive central nervous system depression, leading to profound drowsiness, impaired alertness, and psychomotor deceleration.
text: Primary Medicine: Cetirizine / Levocetirizine. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Alcohol, Sedatives, CNS Depressants. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Additive central nervous system depression, leading to profound drowsiness, impaired alertness, and psychomotor deceleration.

clinical_reason: Hepatic enzyme inducers accelerate the metabolism of montelukast, significantly lowering its therapeutic efficacy for asthma management.
text: Primary Medicine: Montelukast. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Phenobarbital, Rifampin. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Hepatic enzyme inducers accelerate the metabolism of montelukast, significantly lowering its therapeutic efficacy for asthma management.

clinical_reason: Beta-blockers antagonize the effects of salbutamol, completely blocking bronchodilation and potentially triggering severe bronchospasms.
text: Primary Medicine: Salbutamol (Albuterol). Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Non-selective Beta-blockers (such as Carvedilol, Propranolol). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Beta-blockers antagonize the effects of salbutamol, completely blocking bronchodilation and potentially triggering severe bronchospasms.

clinical_reason: High risk of precipitating Serotonin Syndrome, characterized by severe tremors, hyperthermia, and blood pressure spikes.
text: Primary Medicine: Dextromethorphan. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: MAO inhibitors (MAOIs), SSRI antidepressants. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: High risk of precipitating Serotonin Syndrome, characterized by severe tremors, hyperthermia, and blood pressure spikes.

clinical_reason: Amoxicillin reduces the renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity risks. Co-administration with allopurinol significantly increases skin rash rates.
text: Primary Medicine: Amoxicillin. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Methotrexate, Allopurinol. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Amoxicillin reduces the renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing toxicity risks. Co-administration with allopurinol significantly increases skin rash rates.

clinical_reason: Synergistic risk of QT-interval prolongation, potentially causing fatal ventricular arrhythmias like Torsades de Pointes.
text: Primary Medicine: Azithromycin. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Amiodarone, Haloperidol, Ondansetron. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Synergistic risk of QT-interval prolongation, potentially causing fatal ventricular arrhythmias like Torsades de Pointes.

clinical_reason: Divalent/trivalent cations chelate with the antibiotic in the GI tract, drastically reducing oral absorption and causing treatment failure.
text: Primary Medicine: Doxycycline. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Antacids (Magnesium/Aluminum), Iron supplements, Calcium Carbonate, Dairy products. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Divalent/trivalent cations chelate with the antibiotic in the GI tract, drastically reducing oral absorption and causing treatment failure.

clinical_reason: Inhibits CYP1A2, drastically elevating levels of theophylline (toxicity risk) and tizanidine (severe hypotension risk). Subject to chelation by minerals.
text: Primary Medicine: Ciprofloxacin. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Theophylline, Tizanidine, Cation supplements. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Inhibits CYP1A2, drastically elevating levels of theophylline (toxicity risk) and tizanidine (severe hypotension risk). Subject to chelation by minerals.

clinical_reason: Triggers a severe disulfiram-like reaction (nausea, vomiting, throbbing headache, tachycardia). Also significantly enhances the bleeding effect of Warfarin.
text: Primary Medicine: Metronidazole. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Alcohol (and alcohol-containing elixirs), Oral Anticoagulants (Warfarin). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Triggers a severe disulfiram-like reaction (nausea, vomiting, throbbing headache, tachycardia). Also significantly enhances the bleeding effect of Warfarin.

clinical_reason: Significantly raises amlodipine levels (hypotension risk) and increases systemic exposure to simvastatin, boosting myopathy risks.
text: Primary Medicine: Amlodipine. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors (Clarithromycin, Ketoconazole), Simvastatin. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Significantly raises amlodipine levels (hypotension risk) and increases systemic exposure to simvastatin, boosting myopathy risks.

clinical_reason: Severe risk of hyperkalemia (dangerously elevated blood potassium), risking cardiac arrest. NSAIDs blunt effect and threaten renal function.
text: Primary Medicine: Losartan. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Potassium supplements, Spironolactone, NSAIDs. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Severe risk of hyperkalemia (dangerously elevated blood potassium), risking cardiac arrest. NSAIDs blunt effect and threaten renal function.

clinical_reason: Severe risk of additive bradycardia, profound hypotension, and complete heart block.
text: Primary Medicine: Metoprolol. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Calcium Channel Blockers (Verapamil, Diltiazem), CYP2D6 Inhibitors. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Severe risk of additive bradycardia, profound hypotension, and complete heart block.

clinical_reason: Inhibition of clearing pathways drastically raises statin serum levels, maximizing the risk of severe myopathy and fatal rhabdomyolysis.
text: Primary Medicine: Atorvastatin / Rosuvastatin. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Gemfibrozil, Cyclosporine, Erythromycin, Ketoconazole. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Inhibition of clearing pathways drastically raises statin serum levels, maximizing the risk of severe myopathy and fatal rhabdomyolysis.

clinical_reason: Reduces lithium excretion (causing lithium toxicity). Electrolyte imbalances (hypokalemia) increase risk of fatal digoxin toxicity.
text: Primary Medicine: Hydrochlorothiazide. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Lithium, Digoxin, NSAIDs. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Reduces lithium excretion (causing lithium toxicity). Electrolyte imbalances (hypokalemia) increase risk of fatal digoxin toxicity.

clinical_reason: Iodinated contrast can cause acute kidney injury, resulting in metformin accumulation and a potentially fatal buildup of lactic acidosis.
text: Primary Medicine: Metformin. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Iodinated Contrast Media, Topiramate, excessive Alcohol. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Iodinated contrast can cause acute kidney injury, resulting in metformin accumulation and a potentially fatal buildup of lactic acidosis.

clinical_reason: These agents drastically impair the absorption of levothyroxine in the gut, leading to persistent clinical hypothyroidism. Space apart by 4 hours.
text: Primary Medicine: Levothyroxine. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Calcium Carbonate, Iron supplements, Proton Pump Inhibitors. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: These agents drastically impair the absorption of levothyroxine in the gut, leading to persistent clinical hypothyroidism. Space apart by 4 hours.

clinical_reason: Severe risk of gastrointestinal ulcers. Corticosteroids suppress the immune response (risking live vaccine infection) and elevate blood glucose.
text: Primary Medicine: Prednisolone / Prednisone. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: NSAIDs (Ibuprofen), Live Vaccines, Antidiabetic drugs. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Severe risk of gastrointestinal ulcers. Corticosteroids suppress the immune response (risking live vaccine infection) and elevate blood glucose.

clinical_reason: Antacids reduce gabapentin bioavailability by 20% (space out by 2 hours). Opioids cause additive respiratory depression and severe sedation.
text: Primary Medicine: Gabapentin. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Antacids (containing Aluminum/Magnesium), Opioids (Morphine, Tramadol). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Antacids reduce gabapentin bioavailability by 20% (space out by 2 hours). Opioids cause additive respiratory depression and severe sedation.

clinical_reason: The silver component can inactivate the enzymatic debriding action, rendering the enzymatic treatment useless on burns.
text: Primary Medicine: Silver Sulfadiazine. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Topical Proteolytic Enzymes (e.g., Collagenase). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: The silver component can inactivate the enzymatic debriding action, rendering the enzymatic treatment useless on burns.

avoid_with: Active untreated skin infections (viral, fungal, or bacterial)
clinical_reason: Can mask or profoundly worsen active infections due to localized immunosuppression. Overuse risks systemic corticosteroid toxicities.
text: Primary Medicine: Hydrocortisone / Clobetasol. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Other systemic steroids, active untreated skin infections (viral, fungal, or bacterial). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Can mask or profoundly worsen active infections due to localized immunosuppression. Overuse risks systemic corticosteroid toxicities.

clinical_reason: Combining these daily topical agents without careful clinical spacing can trigger severe skin irritation, peeling, erythema, and barrier breakdown.
text: Primary Medicine: Salicylic Acid / Benzoyl Peroxide. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Tretinoin, Retinoids, other aggressive abrasive chemical peels. Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Combining these daily topical agents without careful clinical spacing can trigger severe skin irritation, peeling, erythema, and barrier breakdown.

avoid_with: Topical Corticosteroids (if mixed inappropriately without monitoring)
clinical_reason: Steroids can suppress local immunity, allowing fungal infections to spread if antifungal dosing is insufficient.
text: Primary Medicine: Ketoconazole / Clotrimazole. Do Not Prescribe With / Avoid: Topical Corticosteroids (if mixed inappropriately without monitoring). Clinical Reason / Adverse Risk: Steroids can suppress local immunity, allowing fungal infections to spread if antifungal dosing is insufficient.
